    tp.Env["GC_AGENT"] = identity
    tp.Env["GC_SESSION_ORIGIN"] = "named"
+   tp.Env["BEADS_ACTOR"] = identity

Step 3 — tests. Extend
`cmd/gc/session_model_phase0_runtime_env_spec_test.go` (or add a
new `_test.go` beside it if that file is spec-locked) with one
table-driven test that builds desired state for a configured named
session and asserts:

    tp.Env["BEADS_ACTOR"] == identity
    tp.Env["BEADS_ACTOR"] == tp.Env["GC_AGENT"]
    tp.Env["BEADS_ACTOR"] == tp.Env["GC_TEMPLATE"]   // for singleton named

And one test for a pool/ephemeral agent that asserts:

    tp.Env["BEADS_ACTOR"] == tp.Env["GC_SESSION_NAME"]
    tp.Env["BEADS_ACTOR"] == tp.Env["GC_AGENT"]

The existing test helpers in that file already build `tp` via
`resolveTemplatePrepared` / `buildDesiredState`; follow the same
shape.

Step 4 — docs. Add a short subsection to
`docs/reference/cli.md` under the session-environment / agent-env
documentation (or create one if it doesn't exist yet) titled
"BEADS_ACTOR convention." Content:

  - `BEADS_ACTOR` is the default actor for `bd` mutations, including
    `bd update <id> --claim` when `--assignee` is omitted.
  - For **named singleton** sessions, `BEADS_ACTOR == GC_AGENT ==
    GC_TEMPLATE == <rig>/<template>` (e.g. `gascity/investigator`).
  - For **pool/ephemeral** sessions, `BEADS_ACTOR == GC_SESSION_NAME
    == <template-basename>-<beadID>` (e.g. `designer-lgf-b807`).
  - For **manual** (`gc session new`) sessions without `--alias`,
    `BEADS_ACTOR == GC_SESSION_NAME == s-<beadID>` (e.g.
    `s-lgf-jtvr`).
  - `bd update --claim --assignee=<X>` uses `<X>` literally; the
    `--assignee` flag overrides `BEADS_ACTOR` for that call. Pack
    prompts pin `--assignee="$GC_TEMPLATE"` for stable routing
    regardless of spawn path.

## Out of scope

- Do **not** change the BEADS_ACTOR format for pool or manual
  sessions. The acceptance criteria only pin the named-singleton
  case to `GC_TEMPLATE`; changing the others is a larger refactor.
- Do **not** refactor `RuntimeEnvWithSessionContext` to also set
  BEADS_ACTOR. Today BEADS_ACTOR is set in one place
  (`template_resolve.go`) and the named overrides run after that,
  so adding the line in the named paths is sufficient. Centralizing
  via a helper is a nice cleanup but belongs in a follow-up.
- Do **not** investigate the "sling to `l1-gc-factory/claude-multiplex/builder`
  didn't auto-spawn" note at the bottom of the bead description —
  the bug author explicitly said to record but not investigate now.
- Do **not** touch `template_resolve.go:223` (the ephemeral path
  already sets BEADS_ACTOR correctly for its case).

## Done-when

- [ ] `cmd/gc/build_desired_state.go` named-session loop sets
      `tp.Env["BEADS_ACTOR"] = identity`.
- [ ] `cmd/gc/session_reconciler.go` `resolveTemplateForLiveNamedSession`
      sets `tp.Env["BEADS_ACTOR"] = identity`.
- [ ] New phase0 runtime-env test asserts `BEADS_ACTOR == GC_AGENT ==
      GC_TEMPLATE` for a named singleton `TemplateParams`.
- [ ] New phase0 test asserts `BEADS_ACTOR == GC_SESSION_NAME ==
      GC_AGENT` for an ephemeral-pool `TemplateParams`.
- [ ] `go test ./cmd/gc/... ./internal/session/...` is green.
- [ ] `docs/reference/cli.md` gains a "BEADS_ACTOR convention"
      subsection per Step 4.
- [ ] Manual smoke: after rebuilding `gc` and restarting a named
      session, `cat /proc/$(pgrep -f '\[... \] <rig>/<template>')/environ
      | tr '\0' '\n' | grep -E '^(BEADS_ACTOR|GC_AGENT|GC_TEMPLATE)='`
      shows the same value for all three.
notes: Duplicate of ga-6fo (same investigator dump, created 4 seconds earlier). Closing to avoid double-work.

title: Fix: compactor order — convert from formula+pool to exec, wire up the actual flatten
description:
## Root cause

`mol-dog-compactor` order is declared as `formula = ... pool = "dog"` but the
formula body carries a **"ZFC Exemption"** section asserting agents can't
execute it — a compactor *daemon* is supposed to, and no such daemon exists
anywhere in `gascity` (grepped `mol-dog-compactor`, `CompactDog`, `PourMolecule`,
etc. under `cmd/`, `internal/`, `examples/` — zero hits in Go code outside tests).

Effect on user's city (`gc-management`):

- Order fired 4 times on 2026-04-13/14/15/16. Last firing's cycle bead
  `gm-z4m9crf` ("mol-dog-compactor") is **still OPEN** since 2026-04-16
  (6 days ago as of 2026-04-22), with 5 open child task beads.
- Because the cycle bead never closed, the order is blocked from firing
  again.
- Meanwhile, `mol-dog-reaper` (which was already converted formula+pool →
  exec — see its own `# Converted from formula+pool to exec` header
  comment) has fired 166× over the same window and completes on schedule.
- With no compactor running, `gm` DB ballooned to 9.9 GB (~47k issues).
  User worked around it manually via dump → DROP DATABASE → re-import
  (reclaimed 94% / 9.3 GB), but that procedure is fragile and undocumented.

## Files involved

**Source of the broken order — this is what you change:**

- `gascity/examples/dolt/orders/mol-dog-compactor.toml:1-6`
  (currently: `formula = "mol-dog-compactor"` / `pool = "dog"` /
  `trigger = "cooldown"` / `interval = "24h"`)
- `gascity/examples/dolt/formulas/mol-dog-compactor.toml` (wrapper)
- `gascity/examples/dolt/formulas/mol-dog-compactor.formula.toml`
  (the formula body, with the "ZFC Exemption" section)

**Working model to copy — exec-based order:**

- `gascity/examples/gastown/packs/maintenance/orders/mol-dog-reaper.toml`
  (projected copy lives in the user's city at
  `gc-management/.gc/system/packs/maintenance/orders/mol-dog-reaper.toml`
  — shape is `exec = "$PACK_DIR/assets/scripts/reaper.sh"` + cooldown)
- `gascity/examples/gastown/packs/maintenance/assets/scripts/reaper.sh`
  — pattern for enumerating user DBs and iterating. Also the inline SQL
  pattern using `dolt_sql` and table filtering conventions.

**Primitives that already exist and do the work:**

- `bd flatten` (beads rig) — implements the Tim Sehn
  soft-reset/commit/swap recipe via `CALL DOLT_*` stored procs. Driver
  at `beads/internal/storage/versioncontrolops/flatten.go:22-70`.
  Landed 2026-03-25 (beads `5e0e83ab`), server-mode-safe since
  `c4fbe1ea`.
- `bd compact --dolt` (beads rig) — selective squash preserving recent
  commits. Not needed for this fix but present for reference.

**Additional dependency:**

- Compactor script needs `dolt-target.sh` (port/host discovery). Currently
  lives in the maintenance pack at
  `examples/gastown/packs/maintenance/assets/scripts/dolt-target.sh`.
  The `dolt` pack does not currently import `maintenance`. Pick whichever
  matches existing pack-dependency conventions:
  (a) copy the small script into `dolt/assets/scripts/`,
  (b) add an import dependency on `maintenance`, or
  (c) inline the few lines of port discovery.
  The reaper uses `. "$SCRIPT_DIR/dolt-target.sh"` — keep the same API.

## Reproducing test

Functional repro against the user's city (read-only observation):

```
grep '"type":"order.fired".*mol-dog-compactor' \
  /home/jaword/projects/gc-management/.gc/events.jsonl | wc -l
  # → 4 (last on 2026-04-16)

bd --rig gm list --label 'order-run:mol-dog-compactor' --status=open
  # → gm-z4m9crf (5 open children), stuck for 6 days

grep -c '"type":"order.fired".*mol-dog-reaper' \
  /home/jaword/projects/gc-management/.gc/events.jsonl
  # → 166 — reaper fires on schedule because it's exec-based
```

Acceptance test to add (integration/acceptance suite):

1. Stand up a sandbox city with the dolt pack.
2. Seed a sandbox DB with `> $threshold` commits (default 500).
3. Trigger the order manually (`gc order run mol-dog-compactor` or wait
   for cooldown).
4. Assert within N seconds:
   - The order's exec exit code is 0.
   - `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM dolt_log` on the seeded DB drops to 1.
   - Row counts on every user table are unchanged (integrity check —
     this is the safety guarantee the original formula described).
   - Next firing cycle is not blocked.

## Fix spec

### 1. Convert the order (small, mechanical)

Rewrite `gascity/examples/dolt/orders/mol-dog-compactor.toml` to the
exec shape, mirroring the reaper header comment for provenance:

```toml
# Converted from formula+pool to exec. All compactor operations are
# deterministic: SQL resets, commits, and CALL dolt_gc. No LLM judgment
# needed — runs inline in the controller.
[order]
description = "Flatten Dolt commit history to reduce storage"
exec = "$PACK_DIR/assets/scripts/compactor.sh"
trigger = "cooldown"
interval = "24h"
```

Delete (or tombstone per project norm):

- `examples/dolt/formulas/mol-dog-compactor.toml`
- `examples/dolt/formulas/mol-dog-compactor.formula.toml`

If the files are embedded via `examples/dolt/embed.go`, make sure the
embed directive still compiles after removal (no dangling entries).

### 2. Add `examples/dolt/assets/scripts/compactor.sh`

Model after `reaper.sh`. Rough structure:

```sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# compactor — flatten Dolt history on each user DB to reclaim storage.
#
# Replaces mol-dog-compactor formula. Uses the Tim Sehn flatten recipe
# (soft-reset to root, commit, swap main) via CALL DOLT_* stored procs.
# The DOLT_CHECKOUT/RESET/COMMIT calls are session-stateful — run them
# as one multi-statement query so they share a session.
set -euo pipefail

# Resolve dolt_sql helper (port/host discovery). Either source from
# maintenance pack or duplicate its handful of lines.
. "$(dirname "$0")/dolt-target.sh"

THRESHOLD="${GC_COMPACTOR_THRESHOLD:-500}"
DRY_RUN="${GC_COMPACTOR_DRY_RUN:-}"

DATABASES=$(dolt_sql -r csv -q "SHOW DATABASES" 2>/dev/null \
    | tail -n +2 \
    | grep -vi '^information_schema$\|^mysql$\|^dolt_cluster$\|^__gc_probe$\|^dolt$' \
    || true)
[ -z "$DATABASES" ] && exit 0

for DB in $DATABASES; do
    COMMITS=$(dolt_sql -r csv -q "USE \`$DB\`; SELECT COUNT(*) FROM dolt_log" \
        2>/dev/null | tail -1 || echo 0)
    [ "$COMMITS" -le "$THRESHOLD" ] && continue

    # Pre-flight row counts for integrity check (excluding dolt_* system tables).
    # Capture into a per-DB temp file to compare post-flatten.

    [ -n "$DRY_RUN" ] && { echo "would flatten $DB ($COMMITS commits)"; continue; }

    # Flatten recipe in ONE dolt_sql invocation (single session required).
    dolt_sql <<SQL
USE \`$DB\`;
SET @initial := (SELECT commit_hash FROM dolt_log ORDER BY date ASC LIMIT 1);
CALL DOLT_BRANCH('flatten-tmp');
CALL DOLT_CHECKOUT('flatten-tmp');
CALL DOLT_RESET('--soft', @initial);
CALL DOLT_COMMIT('-Am', 'compaction: flatten history');
CALL DOLT_CHECKOUT('main');
CALL DOLT_RESET('--hard', 'flatten-tmp');
CALL DOLT_BRANCH('-D', 'flatten-tmp');
CALL DOLT_GC();
SQL

    # Post-flight integrity check: row counts unchanged, commit count == 1.
    # On mismatch → escalate: gc mail send mayor/ -s "ESCALATION: ... [CRITICAL]"; exit 1.
done
```

Notes for the builder:

- Keep the script idempotent: same-cycle re-run should be a no-op
  (commits below threshold → skip).
- Flatten is **safe with concurrent writes** per the original formula's
  own claim ("merge base shifts but diff is just the txn"). Do NOT add
  `gc suspend`/`gc resume` in this patch — if live-write safety turns
  out to be wrong, a cfg toggle can follow.
- The DOLT_CHECKOUT mutation only affects the compactor's own session.
  Other concurrent sessions keep their current branch.
- Match the reaper's defensive style (`|| true`, `2>/dev/null`) so a
  single-DB failure doesn't abort the whole cycle.
- `DOLT_GC()` cannot run inside an explicit transaction (matches
  `beads/internal/storage/versioncontrolops/gc.go:8-16`). Keep it as
  the last statement in the same script but outside any BEGIN.
- Empty or already-flat DBs: when `COUNT(*) FROM dolt_log == 1`, skip.

### 3. No beads-rig change needed

`bd flatten` already does this at the per-DB level. We intentionally
**do not** shell out to `bd flatten` from the script because:

- `bd flatten` picks DB from the caller's `.beads/config.yaml` (cwd),
  which forces `cd` into each rig's beads dir.
- The dolt server in a typical gastown city hosts several DBs (the
  user's city has `gm`, `be`, `gascity`, `mc`, `sfi`,
  `projectwrenunity`) — iterating via `dolt sql` against each DB on the
  server is a closer match to how `reaper.sh` already works.
- Keeps the order self-contained (no `bd` binary dependency on the
  controller host).

If the beads team wants a `bd squash` alias for `bd flatten`, that's a
separate trivial PR against `beads/cmd/bd/flatten.go`. Not required
here.

### 4. No dolt-rig change needed

All primitives used (`DOLT_BRANCH`, `DOLT_CHECKOUT`, `DOLT_RESET --soft`,
`DOLT_COMMIT`, `DOLT_GC`) are existing stored procedures in
`dolt/go/libraries/doltcore/sqle/dprocedures/init.go`. The user's prior
attempt at `CALL dolt_checkout('--orphan')` is unsupported — confirmed
via `dolt/go/cmd/dolt/cli/arg_parser_helpers.go:184-193`, which
registers only `-b`, `-B`, `-f`, `-t`, and `--overwrite-ignore`. The
soft-reset path we use does not need orphan support.

## Out of scope

- `bd squash` alias in beads rig (see §3 above).
- `gc suspend`/`gc resume` integration around the flatten (see §2 note).
- Surgical mode (preserve recent N commits via interactive rebase). The
  original formula defined a surgical mode too — carry it over only if
  trivial; flatten alone resolves the immediate bloat. A separate bead
  can add surgical.
- Telemetry / structured report output beyond what reaper currently
  emits.

## Done-when

- [ ] `examples/dolt/assets/scripts/compactor.sh` added, executable,
      passes `shellcheck` and the gascity lint rules that apply to
      `reaper.sh`.
- [ ] `examples/dolt/orders/mol-dog-compactor.toml` rewritten in the
      exec shape above.
- [ ] Formula files removed (or tombstoned per project norm).
- [ ] `examples/dolt/embed.go` still compiles after file changes.
- [ ] Acceptance test (per §Reproducing test) added and green.
- [ ] `go test ./...` green under packages touched.
- [ ] PR opened and merged in `gastownhall/gascity`.
- [ ] **Operator note in PR description** — after merge, the user must
      close the stuck cycle bead in their live city to unblock
      scheduling. Do NOT automate from CI; it's a one-time recovery:

      ```
      cd /home/jaword/projects/gc-management
      for id in gm-z4m9crf gm-z4m9crf.1 gm-z4m9crf.2 \
                gm-z4m9crf.3 gm-z4m9crf.4 gm-z4m9crf.5; do
        bd close "$id" --reason "superseded: compactor order converted to exec"
      done
      ```

## Routing notes for the builder

- Rig: `gascity` (the order and script files are source in
  `gascity/examples/dolt/`, `//go:embed`-ed into the `gc` binary via
  `examples/dolt/embed.go`).
- No beads PR, no dolt PR. Single-rig change.
- Full verification requires the user to rebuild and run `gc` in their
  city — include the operator recovery command in the PR description.
notes:
Implementation complete:
- examples/dolt/assets/scripts/compactor.sh: new exec script with flatten recipe, dry-run, integrity check, escalation to mayor on row-count divergence
- examples/dolt/orders/mol-dog-compactor.toml: converted to exec order
- examples/dolt/formulas/mol-dog-compactor.toml: deleted
- examples/dolt/compactor_test.go: 6 unit tests (projected target, managed port fallback, below-threshold skip, flatten above threshold, dry-run, order shape)
- examples/dolt/assets/scripts/runtime.sh: guard GC_DOLT_PORT under set -u

Test results: all 6 compactor tests green; examples/dolt (11s) and examples/gastown (13s) green; TestBuiltinPackIncludes* green.

Unrelated pre-existing cmd/gc failures (TestCmdOrderHistory/Run*ProviderAware) confirmed on baseline via git stash.

title: Architecture: bd/dolt query latency (~5s) gates all gc reads
description:
Follow-up from ga-amh (gc session list slowness).

## Finding

Every `bd` subprocess call against the city's dolt store takes 4-7 seconds on the current gc-management instance. This is independent of which query shape is asked:

- `bd list --json --label=gc:session` (8 beads returned): 4.75s
- `bd list --json` (221 beads returned): 7.01s
- `bd show --json <id>` (1 bead returned): 5.51s
- `bd list --json --limit 5`: 4.86s

The dolt data directory at `/home/jaword/projects/gc-management/.beads/dolt` is **11 GB** despite storing only 221 issues. This is likely several orders of magnitude of accumulated history relative to the live dataset.

Because bd invocations cost this much, gc session list --json hits a hard ~5s floor no matter how the CLI is rewritten — every status-like command is bounded by a single bd list query.

## Decision needed

The ga-amh acceptance criteria asked for <500ms on gc session list --json. The PR I just opened (#1128) fixes the amplifier (redundant bd/tmux calls per session, 192s → 5s for non-JSON), but it cannot cross the ~5s floor without one of:

1. Store maintenance: dolt gc / archive old history on the managed dolt server. Operational, not code. Would need to be a rigged-in periodic task or manual procedure with a runbook.
2. Supervisor-side status cache: a long-lived process maintains a read model of session state on disk / memory and serves CLI reads from it (e.g. a --cached flag, or the CLI reads the cache when fresh and falls back to bd when stale). New abstraction with its own invalidation rules and failure modes — architect call.
3. Different schema for status: move status-frequently-read fields out of Dolt into a lighter read path (flatfile snapshot, SQLite, etc.). Also an architect call.

All three are outside an investigator's authority — they require a call on what the system should look like, not just a fix to what it is.

## Evidence

- PR #1128 for the amplifier fix: https://github.com/gastownhall/gascity/pull/1128
- Dolt directory size: du -sh /home/jaword/projects/gc-management/.beads/dolt → 11G
- Live issue count: bd list --json --limit 0 | jq length → 221

## Out of scope here

- The per-session redundancy in cmd_session.go / session_reconcile.go is already in #1128; not this bead's concern.
notes:
architect-1 investigation 2026-04-22:

Confirmed and extended the investigator's findings:

1. **Physical store**: .beads/dolt/gm/.dolt/noms/oldgen = 9.8 GB of noms chunks for ~64 issues. Dolt history dominates the store.
2. **Reproduced latency**: `bd list --json --label=gc:session --include-infra --include-gates --limit 0` against the managed gm Dolt server: 5.4-6.0s wall time, consistent. Directly matches gc session list --json floor.
3. **Existing infrastructure worth leveraging**:
   - internal/beads/caching_store.go already wraps BdStore with an in-memory cache + event-driven invalidation (PrimeActive, Prime, StartReconciler). Used by the controller via cmd/gc/api_state.go:wrapWithCachingStore.
   - internal/api (Huma) already exposes /v0/sessions backed by that CachingStore (humaHandleSessionList). Reads come from in-memory state when cache is live.
   - cmd/gc/apiroute.go:apiClient(cityPath) already routes CLI commands to the supervisor's localhost API when the controller is alive, with ShouldFallback-based graceful fallback to raw bd.
   - The apiClient+fallback pattern is already used by: cmd_session submit, cmd_suspend, cmd_agent (pool set/get), cmd_rig (add/update). Mutations.
4. **The gap**: cmdSessionList at cmd/gc/cmd_session.go:515 opens the raw BdStore directly. It does NOT try apiClient first. Same likely holds for other read-path siblings (peek/wake/get, status, convoy/mail reads) — none were changed by PR #1128.

Architecture conclusion: the three options in the bead collapse to TWO parallel decisions. No new abstraction required.

- ADR: read-path CLI commands route through supervisor API (reuses existing CachingStore; bounds gc session list --json well under 500ms when supervisor is up; gracefully falls back to raw bd when down). This is "option 2" but as *wiring*, not a new cache.
- ADR: dolt store maintenance runbook (periodic dolt gc on the managed server + guardrail against orphan probe dbs). This is "option 1"; bounds the cold-path floor (supervisor startup, hooks, operator bd invocations, test fixtures).
- Option 3 (separate schema) NOT pursued — not needed if 1+2 are in place.
architect-1 outputs 2026-04-22:

Architecture doc:
- docs/architecture/gc-read-path.md (FR/NFR, data model erDiagram, 3 use-case flowcharts, 2 sequenceDiagrams with prose, security/integrations/risks tables, alternatives)

ADRs:
- docs/adr/0001-gc-read-path-routes-through-supervisor.md
- docs/adr/0002-dolt-store-maintenance-runbook.md

Guardrail rules:
- docs/rules/gc-read-path-routes-through-api.md
- docs/rules/dolt-store-maintenance.md

adr_sync (actual adr-bot --no-tailor):
- ran successfully against /home/jaword/projects/gascity
- server reports 'No ADRs available for this stack yet' for go/cobra
- result: no managed CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md emitted; locally-authored ADRs in docs/adr/ are the source of truth.

git commit: skipped. Working tree is on user feature branch fix/799-control-dispatcher-pool-resume-flags and no user.name/user.email configured locally. Files left in working tree (untracked) for downstream agents and the user to commit on a clean branch when appropriate.

Children ga-h6w (ADR 0001 read-path routing) and ga-d5y (ADR 0002 dolt maintenance) created with needs-design label and slung to gascity/designer. See ga-1f9 notes for output paths and adr-bot sync result.

title: PR 1026: address Julian's review comments
description:
GitHub PR: https://github.com/gastownhall/gascity/pull/1026

Julian requested changes with three points (see the PR comments for full text):

1. **Major**: the new stale-sibling warning leaks into opportunistic resolveCity() fallback paths. Unrelated commands emit the warning when probing outside a city. Fix: suppress the warning when failOnLoadError=false, OR move emission up to callers that explicitly depend on registered-rig resolution.

2. **Minor**: warning reporting lives inside registeredRigBindings via a package-global writer. Bypasses command-owned stderr, can duplicate the same warning in one command, forces tests to override global state. Fix: route diagnostics through caller-owned output or structured results; emit each stale entry at most once per command.

3. **Minor**: the stale-entry tolerance is a Stat-then-load check — TOCTOU race. If sibling city.toml disappears between Stat and loadCityConfigSuppressDeprecatedOrderWarnings, ENOENT still lands in loadErrors and aborts. Fix: handle os.ErrNotExist on the actual config-load path and add regression coverage.

Branch is whichever PR 1026 points at; discover with 'gh pr view 1026 --repo gastownhall/gascity --json headRefName'. Push to same branch. Do NOT merge. Reply on GitHub when done.
acceptance_criteria: 1) All three review points addressed with code changes + tests in the feature branch. 2) Tests pass locally. 3) Branch pushed to origin. 4) Reply comment posted on PR 1026 enumerating each point and the commit that addressed it. 5) Bead closed with PR URL and commit shas in notes.
notes: PR 1026: addressed all 3 review points in commit 269453d6 (pushed to fork). Replied on GitHub: https://github.com/gastownhall/gascity/pull/1026#issuecomment-4300195278

title: Close molecule and signal completion
description:
Work is done. Clean up structural beads, then drain.

**1. Close the mol-do-work molecule parent:**
```bash
bd show ga-dr0
```
Look at the PARENT section. The mol-do-work molecule bead (e.g., `ga-dr0.1`) should
be closed now that all steps are complete:
```bash
bd update <molecule-parent-id> --status=closed --notes "all steps completed"
```

**2. Close the convoy/sling parent if one exists:**
Check if the molecule parent itself has a parent (a `sling-*` convoy bead). If so, close it:
```bash
bd update <convoy-id> --status=closed --notes "dispatch completed"
```

**3. Drain:**
```bash
gc runtime drain-ack
```

title: Read assignment, implement, and close
description:
You have been assigned a work bead. Read it, do the work, and close it.

**1. Read your assignment:**
```bash
bd show ga-dr0
```

Read the bead's title and description carefully. This is your task.

**2. Implement the solution:**

Do exactly what the bead describes. Follow existing codebase conventions.
Make atomic, focused commits as you work:
```bash
git add <files>
git commit -m "<type>: <description>"
```

**3. Close the bead when done:**
```bash
bd update ga-dr0 --status=closed --notes "Done: <brief summary of what you did>"
```

If you get stuck or need clarification, check your inbox:
```bash
gc mail inbox
```

**Exit criteria:** The work described in the bead is complete and the bead is closed.

title: mol-do-work
description:
Simple work formula — read the bead, do what it says, close it.

This is the minimal work lifecycle for coding agents. No git branching,
no worktree isolation, no refinery handoff. The agent reads the bead's
description, implements the solution in the current working directory,
and closes the bead when done.

Use this for demos and simple single-agent workflows. For production
multi-agent setups with branch isolation and merge review, use
mol-polecat-work instead.

title: TDD: fix gascity #793
description:
Track GH issue gastownhall/gascity#793: scale_check / work_query commands are not template-expanded — `{{.Rig}}` passed verbatim, breaks rig-scoped pool agents.

## The bug
scale_check and work_query fields in agent config accept template vars like `{{.Rig}}` but the substitution is not actually run — the literal string `{{.Rig}}` is passed to the command and breaks rig-scoped pool queries.

## Constraints
- **TDD**: test that an agent with `scale_check = "bd list --rig {{.Rig}} ..."` expands to the rig name at invocation time.
- Fix on PR branch; never push to main directly.

## Starting points
- scale_check / work_query invocation path — grep internal/
- Existing template expansion infra for agent args (the one that DOES work for dir, workDir, etc.)

## Deliverable
PR with regression test + fix + references #793. New bugs → new issues.

## HARD RULE — READ CAREFULLY
**You MUST NOT merge to main.** Open a PR and STOP. Do not run `gh pr merge`, do not squash-merge, do not push to main, do not add yourself as a reviewer. A human will review and merge. If a push to main is the only apparent option, abort and leave the PR for human review.
notes: Already fixed by PR #860 (commits 173a0785, 03ae26a6) which added expandAgentCommandTemplate and wired it into the scale_check and work_query hot paths in build_desired_state.go and work_query_probe.go. Verified: tests in cmd/gc/probe_template_test.go cover {{.Rig}}, {{.AgentBase}}, literal-byte-identical, parse error, unknown field, and nil agent cases — all pass on feat/completion-dynamic-args. GH issue #793 is CLOSED.

title: TDD: fix gascity #720
description:
Track GH issue gastownhall/gascity#720: witness salvage + resling leaves zombie molecules; sling has no source-bead mutex, allowing parallel polecats on the same bead.

## The bug
Two coupled problems:
1. Witness "salvage" operation can re-sling a bead while the original molecule is still running → zombie molecule + parallel polecats.
2. `gc sling` has no per-source-bead mutex, so two concurrent slings can race on the same bead.

## Constraints
- **TDD**: two tests — (a) witness salvage while molecule active must no-op or cleanly cancel old molecule; (b) concurrent sling of same bead must serialize (one wins, one skips/waits).
- Fix on PR branch; never push to main directly.

## Starting points
- Witness salvage logic — grep internal/ for "salvage"
- `gc sling` command handler in cmd_sling.go
- Bead-level advisory lock pattern

## Deliverable
PR with 2 regression tests + both fixes + references #720. New bugs → new issues.

## HARD RULE — READ CAREFULLY
**You MUST NOT merge to main.** Open a PR and STOP. Do not run `gh pr merge`, do not squash-merge, do not push to main, do not add yourself as a reviewer. A human will review and merge. If a push to main is the only apparent option, abort and leave the PR for human review.
notes: Already resolved by PR #852 (merged 2026-04-18), which added internal/sourceworkflow with live-workflow singleton (ListLiveRoots) and per-source-bead cross-process lock (WithLock). GH #720 closed 2026-04-19. Both required regression tests exist and pass on main: (a) TestSlingAttachGraphFormulaRejectsExistingLiveRoot verifies a second sling on a source bead with a live workflow returns ConflictError (covers witness salvage no-op / no zombie molecules). (b) TestSlingAttachGraphFormulaConcurrentLaunchCreatesSingleRoot verifies two concurrent AttachFormula calls on the same source produce exactly one live root. Verified: go test ./internal/sourceworkflow/... and the targeted sling tests all PASS. No code changes needed; no PR opened.

title: TDD: fix gascity #799
description: PR #1125 opened with regression test + fix for #799 control-dispatcher pool resume flags

title: TDD: fix gascity #774
description:
Track GH issue gastownhall/gascity#774: {{.AgentBase}} resolves to template name, not namepool slot — pool instances collide on one worktree path.

## The bug
Template substitution of `{{.AgentBase}}` returns the template's base name, not the per-instance namepool slot (e.g. "opus-1", "opus-2"). Multiple pool instances therefore share a single worktree path, stomping each other's work.

## Constraints
- **TDD**: table-driven test that instantiates N pool workers off one template, asserts each gets a UNIQUE worktree path derived from namepool slot.
- Fix on PR branch; never push to main directly.
- Understand the difference between AgentBase, AgentName, and namepool slot/instance identifiers. Fix the template var to use the right one.

## Starting points
- Grep `\.AgentBase` in internal/
- Template expansion code path
- Namepool slot allocation — probably in internal/pool/ or similar

## Deliverable
PR with regression test + fix + references #774. New bugs → new issues.

## HARD RULE — READ CAREFULLY
**You MUST NOT merge to main.** Open a PR and STOP. Do not run `gh pr merge`, do not squash-merge, do not push to main, do not add yourself as a reviewer. A human will review and merge. If a push to main is the only apparent option, abort and leave the PR for human review.
close_reason: Regression test added via PR gastownhall/gascity#1121. Underlying fix shipped in PR #847.

title: TDD: fix gascity #744
description:
Track GH issue gastownhall/gascity#744: gc handoff self-handoff crashes human-attended (named) sessions on PreCompact.

## The bug
When a human-attended named session (e.g. mayor) hits PreCompact, the configured hook runs `gc handoff "context cycle"` to restart itself. This crashes the human's claude session under some conditions.

## Constraints
- **TDD**: reproduce the crash in a test first — simulate PreCompact event on a named session with handoff hook, assert session survives cleanly.
- Fix on PR branch; never push to main directly.
- Preserve handoff semantics for autonomous sessions. Only fix the named-session-attended path.

## Starting points
- Grep `gc handoff` callers and `PreCompact` matcher
- `cmd/gc/cmd_handoff.go` likely
- Settings: hooks/claude.json has `PreCompact` entry running `gc handoff "context cycle"`

## Deliverable
PR with regression test + fix + references #744. New bugs → new issues.

## HARD RULE — READ CAREFULLY
**You MUST NOT merge to main.** Open a PR and STOP. Do not run `gh pr merge`, do not squash-merge, do not push to main, do not add yourself as a reviewer. A human will review and merge. If a push to main is the only apparent option, abort and leave the PR for human review.
close_reason: No-op: #744 already fixed on main via merged PR #927 + follow-up 84c0e19d. Regression test TestDoHandoff_Regression744_NamedSessionSkipsRestart passes. Bead created 2026-04-18, PR merged 2026-04-19 - stale assignment.

title: TDD: fix gascity #663
description:
Track GH issue gastownhall/gascity#663: supervisor restarts entire city on transient Dolt read failure (packets.go:58 unexpected EOF).

## The bug
When reconciler encounters transient Dolt error during session-bead metadata write, current code issues `op=interrupt wave=0` to EVERY managed session (dogs, polecats, deacon, witness, mayor), then restarts the city. 73 occurrences observed in 37h. Each restart takes ~65s and loses in-flight work.

## Constraints
- **TDD**: write a failing test FIRST — inject `unexpected EOF` on ONE SetMetadataBatch call with a fake/wrapped store, assert supervisor retries-or-skips rather than interrupting unrelated sessions.
- Fix on a PR branch against gastownhall/gascity main. **Never push to main — always PR.**
- Target blast radius: isolate per-bead failures. Full city restart OK only if Dolt is truly down (port unreachable for N sec after retry).
- Do NOT fix the EOF root cause (tracked in #525/#560). Make supervisor resilient.

## Starting points
- `cmd/gc/session_beads.go:1136` — setMetaBatch emits "session beads: setting metadata on %s: %v"
- `cmd/gc/session_lifecycle_parallel.go:97` — "session lifecycle: op=%s wave=%d" emitter. Trace callers back to find the decision to interrupt-all.
- `internal/api/client.go` already has `isTransientDoltError` — reusable.

## Deliverable
PR on gastownhall/gascity with: regression test + per-bead retry/skip logic + `make check` passes + references #663. Any NEW bugs uncovered → open separate issues.

## HARD RULE — READ CAREFULLY
**You MUST NOT merge to main.** Open a PR and STOP. Do not run `gh pr merge`, do not squash-merge, do not push to main, do not add yourself as a reviewer. A human will review and merge. If a push to main is the only apparent option, abort and leave the PR for human review.
notes: Opened https://github.com/gastownhall/gascity/pull/1123 with regression test codifying per-bead SetMetadataBatch isolation on transient unexpected-EOF. Adds eofOnBeadStore wrapper + TestSyncSessionBeads_IsolatesSetMetadataBatchEOFToSingleBead; asserts stderr logs the failed bead ID, unrelated beads still have template backfilled, and the failed bead stays unwritten for next-tick retry. Production cascade path was already fixed by PR #937 (safeTick panic recovery); this test guards the companion skip-via-continue contract in syncSessionBeads so future refactors cannot regress it. No production code change. make check pre-existing failures (TestCityRuntimeReload*) reproduce on clean main and are unrelated.

description: test

title: evaluate: gascity pack system docs coherence
description:
## Task

Read engdocs/design/pack-* and packs/README-like files. Compare against actual pack.toml + schema files in packs/. Identify:
1. Design intent vs. current implementation drift
2. Undocumented pack features
3. Concepts that appear in docs but no longer exist

No code changes, no doc changes — evaluation report only.

title: Push feature branch and open PR
description:
Push your feature branch to origin and open a PR against base_branch.
You cease to exist after this step.

**1. Final clean-state verification (safeguard):**
```bash
git status --porcelain
```
If ANY output (untracked files, uncommitted changes):
```bash
git add -A && git commit -m "chore: capture remaining work (ga-62x)"
```

**2. Rebase on latest base_branch (one last time) to minimize PR merge conflicts:**
```bash
git fetch origin main
git rebase origin/main
# If rebase conflicts: resolve them, then git rebase --continue. If
# conflicts are unresolvable, mail witness and drain-ack.
```

**3. Push the feature branch:**
```bash
BRANCH=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)
git push -u origin "$BRANCH"
```

If push fails, mail witness and drain-ack:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Push failed" -m "Issue: ga-62x. Branch: $BRANCH. Cannot push to origin."
gc runtime drain-ack
exit 1
```

**4. Open the PR via gh:**

Derive a title and body from the bead. Keep the title short (≤70 chars);
put detail in the body.
```bash
BEAD_TITLE=$(bd show ga-62x --json | jq -r '.title')
BEAD_ID="ga-62x"

# Title: "<bead title> (<bead id>)" — truncate if too long
PR_TITLE="${BEAD_TITLE} (${BEAD_ID})"

# Body: ID + full description + footer
PR_BODY=$(bd show "$BEAD_ID" --json | jq -r '.description // ""')

gh pr create --base "main" --head "$BRANCH" --title "$PR_TITLE" --body "$(cat <<EOF
$PR_BODY

---

Closes $BEAD_ID (polecat-pr).
EOF
)"
```

Capture the PR URL for the close-note:
```bash
PR_URL=$(gh pr view --json url -q '.url' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
```

If `gh pr create` fails, mail witness and drain-ack:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: gh pr create failed" -m "Issue: ga-62x. Branch: $BRANCH. PR did not open."
gc runtime drain-ack
exit 1
```

**5. Close the bead with a pointer to the PR:**
```bash
bd update ga-62x --notes "PR opened: $PR_URL (branch: $BRANCH)"
bd close ga-62x
```

**6. Tear down the worktree.**

The worktree lives on until here so restarts during push/PR can resume.
Now it's safe to remove:
```bash
WORKTREE_PATH=$(pwd)
cd ..
git worktree remove "$WORKTREE_PATH" --force || rm -rf "$WORKTREE_PATH"
bd update ga-62x --unset-metadata work_dir
# Leave metadata.branch intact — it's useful post-merge for traceability.
```

**7. Signal reconciler and exit.**
```bash
gc runtime drain-ack
exit
```

`gc runtime drain-ack` tells the reconciler to kill this session. The
reconciler only restarts you if the pool check command finds more work.
You are GONE. Done means gone. There is no idle state.

**Exit criteria:** Feature branch pushed, PR opened against
main, worktree removed, bead closed, session exited.

title: Self-review and run tests
description:
Review your changes and verify they work.

**Config: setup_command = **
**Config: typecheck_command = **
**Config: lint_command = **
**Config: build_command = **
**Config: test_command = **

**1. Review the diff:**
```bash
git diff origin/main...HEAD
git log --oneline origin/main..HEAD
git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD
```

Check for: bugs, security issues, style violations, missing error handling,
debug cruft, unintended file changes. Fix anything you find.

**2. Run quality checks (skip empty commands):**
```bash





```

**ALL CHECKS MUST PASS.** If your change caused the failure, fix it.
If pre-existing, file a bead.

**3. Ensure everything is committed:**
```bash
git status                  # Must be clean
git log origin/main..HEAD --oneline  # Must show your commits
```

If uncommitted changes exist:
```bash
git add -A && git commit -m "<type>: <description> (ga-62x)"
```

NEVER discard implementation changes with `git checkout -- .`

**Exit criteria:** All checks pass, all changes committed, working tree clean.

title: Implement the solution
description:
Do the actual implementation work.

**Working principles:**
- Follow existing codebase conventions
- Make atomic, focused commits
- Keep changes scoped to the assigned issue
- Don't gold-plate or scope-creep

**If resuming a rejected branch:** Read `metadata.rejection_reason`
from load-context. Focus on fixing the specific issue that caused
rejection — don't redo everything.

**Commit frequently:**
```bash
git add <files>
git commit -m "<type>: <description> (ga-62x)"
```

Commit types: feat, fix, refactor, test, docs, chore

**Discovered work (outside scope):**
```bash
bd create --title "Found: <description>" --type bug --priority 2
```
Do NOT fix unrelated issues in this branch.

**If stuck (>15 minutes):**
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Stuck on implementation" -m "Issue: ga-62x
Problem: <what's blocking you>
Tried: <what you've attempted>"
```

**If context filling up:**
```bash
gc runtime request-restart
```
This blocks until the controller kills your session. The next session

title: Set up worktree on a feature branch
description:
Create an isolated worktree checked out on a new feature branch off
base_branch. The feature branch will be pushed and turned into a PR
in the terminal step.

**Config: base_branch = main**
**Config: setup_command = **

**1. Fetch latest:**
```bash
git fetch --prune origin
```

**2. Resolve worktree path and branch name from metadata (resume case).**

Check if `metadata.work_dir` already records your worktree path, and if
`metadata.branch` records your feature branch name:
```bash
WORKTREE=$(bd show ga-62x --json | jq -r '.metadata.work_dir // empty')
BRANCH=$(bd show ga-62x --json | jq -r '.metadata.branch // empty')
```

**If worktree path exists in metadata and directory still exists** — reuse it:
```bash
cd "$WORKTREE"
git fetch origin main
# If branch has unpushed local work, rebase it on base_branch; else stay on branch
git rebase origin/main
```
If the directory is missing, fall through to create a new one.

**If no worktree** — create one:
```bash
# Derive a branch name if not already recorded
if [ -z "$BRANCH" ]; then
    SLUG=$(echo "ga-62x" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | tr -c 'a-z0-9-' '-' | sed 's/-\+/-/g; s/^-//; s/-$//')
    BRANCH="users/${GC_AGENT:-polecat}/${SLUG}"
fi

WORKTREE_PATH=$(mktemp -d /tmp/polecat-ga-62x-XXXXXX)
rmdir "$WORKTREE_PATH"
# Create feature branch off latest base_branch, check out in the new worktree
git worktree add -b "$BRANCH" "$WORKTREE_PATH" "origin/main"
cd "$WORKTREE_PATH"
```

Record immediately so restarts and witness recovery can find both:
```bash
bd update ga-62x --set-metadata work_dir="$WORKTREE_PATH"
bd update ga-62x --set-metadata branch="$BRANCH"
```

**3. Ensure clean working state:**
```bash
git status                  # Should be clean
git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD   # Should show your feature branch
```


title: mol-polecat-pr
description:
Polecat PR-review variant — commits to a feature branch and opens a pull
request against base_branch.

Extends mol-polecat-base with a feature-branch workspace setup (isolated
/tmp worktree + branch off base_branch) and a commit-push-PR terminal
step instead of direct-to-base commit. Designed for cities that use
human or automated PR review (e.g., MCDClient) before merge.

## Polecat Contract (PR-Review Model)

1. Receive work (molecule poured with this formula, assigned to you)
2. Follow steps in order (read descriptions, execute, move to next)
3. Commit to feature branch, push, open PR, close bead, exit
4. You are GONE — review + merge happens downstream

**Feature branch + PR.** Work lands on `users/<agent>/<issue-slug>`
branch in a /tmp worktree that is isolated from the shared rig tree.
The PR is opened against base_branch via `gh pr create`.

## Why /tmp worktrees

Pool agents sharing a single rig working tree stomp each other: one
agent's `git checkout <branch>` wipes another agent's untracked files
or changes HEAD out from under them. An isolated `/tmp/polecat-*`
worktree per issue removes the stomping entirely.

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Tests fail | Fix them. Do not proceed with failures. |
| Push fails | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| `gh pr create` fails | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| Blocked on external | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| Context filling | `gc runtime request-restart` (blocks until controller kills you) |
| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |

title: Push feature branch and open PR
description:
Push your feature branch to origin and open a PR against base_branch.
You cease to exist after this step.

**1. Final clean-state verification (safeguard):**
```bash
git status --porcelain
```
If ANY output (untracked files, uncommitted changes):
```bash
git add -A && git commit -m "chore: capture remaining work (ga-x1q)"
```

**2. Rebase on latest base_branch (one last time) to minimize PR merge conflicts:**
```bash
git fetch origin main
git rebase origin/main
# If rebase conflicts: resolve them, then git rebase --continue. If
# conflicts are unresolvable, mail witness and drain-ack.
```

**3. Push the feature branch:**
```bash
BRANCH=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)
git push -u origin "$BRANCH"
```

If push fails, mail witness and drain-ack:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Push failed" -m "Issue: ga-x1q. Branch: $BRANCH. Cannot push to origin."
gc runtime drain-ack
exit 1
```

**4. Open the PR via gh:**

Derive a title and body from the bead. Keep the title short (≤70 chars);
put detail in the body.
```bash
BEAD_TITLE=$(bd show ga-x1q --json | jq -r '.title')
BEAD_ID="ga-x1q"

# Title: "<bead title> (<bead id>)" — truncate if too long
PR_TITLE="${BEAD_TITLE} (${BEAD_ID})"

# Body: ID + full description + footer
PR_BODY=$(bd show "$BEAD_ID" --json | jq -r '.description // ""')

gh pr create --base "main" --head "$BRANCH" --title "$PR_TITLE" --body "$(cat <<EOF
$PR_BODY

---

Closes $BEAD_ID (polecat-pr).
EOF
)"
```

Capture the PR URL for the close-note:
```bash
PR_URL=$(gh pr view --json url -q '.url' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
```

If `gh pr create` fails, mail witness and drain-ack:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: gh pr create failed" -m "Issue: ga-x1q. Branch: $BRANCH. PR did not open."
gc runtime drain-ack
exit 1
```

**5. Close the bead with a pointer to the PR:**
```bash
bd update ga-x1q --notes "PR opened: $PR_URL (branch: $BRANCH)"
bd close ga-x1q
```

**6. Tear down the worktree.**

The worktree lives on until here so restarts during push/PR can resume.
Now it's safe to remove:
```bash
WORKTREE_PATH=$(pwd)
cd ..
git worktree remove "$WORKTREE_PATH" --force || rm -rf "$WORKTREE_PATH"
bd update ga-x1q --unset-metadata work_dir
# Leave metadata.branch intact — it's useful post-merge for traceability.
```

**7. Signal reconciler and exit.**
```bash
gc runtime drain-ack
exit
```

`gc runtime drain-ack` tells the reconciler to kill this session. The
reconciler only restarts you if the pool check command finds more work.
You are GONE. Done means gone. There is no idle state.

**Exit criteria:** Feature branch pushed, PR opened against
main, worktree removed, bead closed, session exited.

title: mol-polecat-pr
description:
Polecat PR-review variant — commits to a feature branch and opens a pull
request against base_branch.

Extends mol-polecat-base with a feature-branch workspace setup (isolated
/tmp worktree + branch off base_branch) and a commit-push-PR terminal
step instead of direct-to-base commit. Designed for cities that use
human or automated PR review (e.g., MCDClient) before merge.

## Polecat Contract (PR-Review Model)

1. Receive work (molecule poured with this formula, assigned to you)
2. Follow steps in order (read descriptions, execute, move to next)
3. Commit to feature branch, push, open PR, close bead, exit
4. You are GONE — review + merge happens downstream

**Feature branch + PR.** Work lands on `users/<agent>/<issue-slug>`
branch in a /tmp worktree that is isolated from the shared rig tree.
The PR is opened against base_branch via `gh pr create`.

## Why /tmp worktrees

Pool agents sharing a single rig working tree stomp each other: one
agent's `git checkout <branch>` wipes another agent's untracked files
or changes HEAD out from under them. An isolated `/tmp/polecat-*`
worktree per issue removes the stomping entirely.

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Tests fail | Fix them. Do not proceed with failures. |
| Push fails | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| `gh pr create` fails | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| Blocked on external | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| Context filling | `gc runtime request-restart` (blocks until controller kills you) |
| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |

title: Push feature branch and open PR
description:
Push your feature branch to origin and open a PR against base_branch.
You cease to exist after this step.

**1. Final clean-state verification (safeguard):**
```bash
git status --porcelain
```
If ANY output (untracked files, uncommitted changes):
```bash
git add -A && git commit -m "chore: capture remaining work (ga-m0m)"
```

**2. Rebase on latest base_branch (one last time) to minimize PR merge conflicts:**
```bash
git fetch origin main
git rebase origin/main
# If rebase conflicts: resolve them, then git rebase --continue. If
# conflicts are unresolvable, mail witness and drain-ack.
```

**3. Push the feature branch:**
```bash
BRANCH=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)
git push -u origin "$BRANCH"
```

If push fails, mail witness and drain-ack:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Push failed" -m "Issue: ga-m0m. Branch: $BRANCH. Cannot push to origin."
gc runtime drain-ack
exit 1
```

**4. Open the PR via gh:**

Derive a title and body from the bead. Keep the title short (≤70 chars);
put detail in the body.
```bash
BEAD_TITLE=$(bd show ga-m0m --json | jq -r '.title')
BEAD_ID="ga-m0m"

# Title: "<bead title> (<bead id>)" — truncate if too long
PR_TITLE="${BEAD_TITLE} (${BEAD_ID})"

# Body: ID + full description + footer
PR_BODY=$(bd show "$BEAD_ID" --json | jq -r '.description // ""')

gh pr create --base "main" --head "$BRANCH" --title "$PR_TITLE" --body "$(cat <<EOF
$PR_BODY

---

Closes $BEAD_ID (polecat-pr).
EOF
)"
```

Capture the PR URL for the close-note:
```bash
PR_URL=$(gh pr view --json url -q '.url' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
```

If `gh pr create` fails, mail witness and drain-ack:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: gh pr create failed" -m "Issue: ga-m0m. Branch: $BRANCH. PR did not open."
gc runtime drain-ack
exit 1
```

**5. Close the bead with a pointer to the PR:**
```bash
bd update ga-m0m --notes "PR opened: $PR_URL (branch: $BRANCH)"
bd close ga-m0m
```

**6. Tear down the worktree.**

The worktree lives on until here so restarts during push/PR can resume.
Now it's safe to remove:
```bash
WORKTREE_PATH=$(pwd)
cd ..
git worktree remove "$WORKTREE_PATH" --force || rm -rf "$WORKTREE_PATH"
bd update ga-m0m --unset-metadata work_dir
# Leave metadata.branch intact — it's useful post-merge for traceability.
```

**7. Signal reconciler and exit.**
```bash
gc runtime drain-ack
exit
```

`gc runtime drain-ack` tells the reconciler to kill this session. The
reconciler only restarts you if the pool check command finds more work.
You are GONE. Done means gone. There is no idle state.

**Exit criteria:** Feature branch pushed, PR opened against
main, worktree removed, bead closed, session exited.

```bash
cd "$WORKTREE_PATH"
```

Record immediately so restarts and witness recovery can find both:

```bash
bd update ga-m0m --set-metadata branch="$BRANCH"
```

branch in a /tmp worktree that is isolated from the shared rig tree.
The PR is opened against base_branch via `gh pr create`.

## Why /tmp worktrees

Pool agents sharing a single rig working tree stomp each other: one
agent's `git checkout <branch>` wipes another agent's untracked files
or changes HEAD out from under them. An isolated `/tmp/polecat-*`
worktree per issue removes the stomping entirely.

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Tests fail | Fix them. Do not proceed with failures. |
| Push fails | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| `gh pr create` fails | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| Blocked on external | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| Context filling | `gc runtime request-restart` (blocks until controller kills you) |
| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |

title: Push feature branch and open PR
description:
Push your feature branch to origin and open a PR against base_branch.
You cease to exist after this step.

**1. Final clean-state verification (safeguard):**
```bash
git status --porcelain
```
If ANY output (untracked files, uncommitted changes):
```bash
git add -A && git commit -m "chore: capture remaining work (ga-uxi)"
```

**2. Rebase on latest base_branch (one last time) to minimize PR merge conflicts:**
```bash
git fetch origin main
git rebase origin/main
# If rebase conflicts: resolve them, then git rebase --continue. If
# conflicts are unresolvable, mail witness and drain-ack.
```

**3. Push the feature branch:**
```bash
BRANCH=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)
git push -u origin "$BRANCH"
```

If push fails, mail witness and drain-ack:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Push failed" -m "Issue: ga-uxi. Branch: $BRANCH. Cannot push to origin."
gc runtime drain-ack
exit 1
```

**4. Open the PR via gh:**

Derive a title and body from the bead. Keep the title short (≤70 chars);
put detail in the body.
```bash
BEAD_TITLE=$(bd show ga-uxi --json | jq -r '.title')
BEAD_ID="ga-uxi"

# Title: "<bead title> (<bead id>)" — truncate if too long
PR_TITLE="${BEAD_TITLE} (${BEAD_ID})"

# Body: ID + full description + footer
PR_BODY=$(bd show "$BEAD_ID" --json | jq -r '.description // ""')

gh pr create --base "main" --head "$BRANCH" --title "$PR_TITLE" --body "$(cat <<EOF
$PR_BODY

---

Closes $BEAD_ID (polecat-pr).
EOF
)"
```

Capture the PR URL for the close-note:
```bash
PR_URL=$(gh pr view --json url -q '.url' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
```

If `gh pr create` fails, mail witness and drain-ack:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: gh pr create failed" -m "Issue: ga-uxi. Branch: $BRANCH. PR did not open."
gc runtime drain-ack
exit 1
```

**5. Close the bead with a pointer to the PR:**
```bash
bd update ga-uxi --notes "PR opened: $PR_URL (branch: $BRANCH)"
bd close ga-uxi
```

**6. Tear down the worktree.**

The worktree lives on until here so restarts during push/PR can resume.
Now it's safe to remove:
```bash
WORKTREE_PATH=$(pwd)
cd ..
git worktree remove "$WORKTREE_PATH" --force || rm -rf "$WORKTREE_PATH"
bd update ga-uxi --unset-metadata work_dir
# Leave metadata.branch intact — it's useful post-merge for traceability.
```

**7. Signal reconciler and exit.**
```bash
gc runtime drain-ack
exit
```

`gc runtime drain-ack` tells the reconciler to kill this session. The
reconciler only restarts you if the pool check command finds more work.
You are GONE. Done means gone. There is no idle state.

**Exit criteria:** Feature branch pushed, PR opened against
main, worktree removed, bead closed, session exited.

title: Set up worktree on a feature branch
description:
Create an isolated worktree checked out on a new feature branch off
base_branch. The feature branch will be pushed and turned into a PR
in the terminal step.

**Config: base_branch = main**
**Config: setup_command = **

**1. Fetch latest:**
```bash
git fetch --prune origin
```

**2. Resolve worktree path and branch name from metadata (resume case).**

Check if `metadata.work_dir` already records your worktree path, and if
`metadata.branch` records your feature branch name:
```bash

BRANCH=$(bd show ga-uxi --json | jq -r '.metadata.branch // empty')
```

**If worktree path exists in metadata and directory still exists** — reuse it:
```bash
cd "$WORKTREE"
git fetch origin main
# If branch has unpushed local work, rebase it on base_branch; else stay on branch
git rebase origin/main
```
If the directory is missing, fall through to create a new one.

**If no worktree** — create one:
```bash
# Derive a branch name if not already recorded
if [ -z "$BRANCH" ]; then
    SLUG=$(echo "ga-uxi" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | tr -c 'a-z0-9-' '-' | sed 's/-\+/-/g; s/^-//; s/-$//')
    BRANCH="users/${GC_AGENT:-polecat}/${SLUG}"
fi

WORKTREE_PATH=$(mktemp -d /tmp/polecat-ga-uxi-XXXXXX)
rmdir "$WORKTREE_PATH"
# Create feature branch off latest base_branch, check out in the new worktree
git worktree add -b "$BRANCH" "$WORKTREE_PATH" "origin/main"
cd "$WORKTREE_PATH"
```

Record immediately so restarts and witness recovery can find both:
```bash
bd update ga-uxi --set-metadata work_dir="$WORKTREE_PATH"
bd update ga-uxi --set-metadata branch="$BRANCH"
```

**3. Ensure clean working state:**
```bash
git status                  # Should be clean
git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD   # Should show your feature branch
```

**4. Run project setup (if configured):**
```bash

```

```bash
PR_BODY=$(bd show "$BEAD_ID" --json | jq -r '.description // ""')

gh pr create --base "main" --head "$BRANCH" --title "$PR_TITLE" --body "$(cat <<EOF
$PR_BODY

---

Closes $BEAD_ID (polecat-pr).
EOF
)"
```

Capture the PR URL for the close-note:
```bash
PR_URL=$(gh pr view --json url -q '.url' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
```

If `gh pr create` fails, mail witness and drain-ack:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: gh pr create failed" -m "Issue: ga-bxq. Branch: $BRANCH. PR did not open."
gc runtime drain-ack
exit 1
```

**5. Close the bead with a pointer to the PR:**
```bash
bd update ga-bxq --notes "PR opened: $PR_URL (branch: $BRANCH)"
bd close ga-bxq
```

**6. Tear down the worktree.**

The worktree lives on until here so restarts during push/PR can resume.
Now it's safe to remove:
```bash
WORKTREE_PATH=$(pwd)
cd ..
git worktree remove "$WORKTREE_PATH" --force || rm -rf "$WORKTREE_PATH"
bd update ga-bxq --unset-metadata work_dir
# Leave metadata.branch intact — it's useful post-merge for traceability.
```

**7. Signal reconciler and exit.**
```bash
gc runtime drain-ack
exit
```

`gc runtime drain-ack` tells the reconciler to kill this session. The
reconciler only restarts you if the pool check command finds more work.
You are GONE. Done means gone. There is no idle state.

**Exit criteria:** Feature branch pushed, PR opened against
main, worktree removed, bead closed, session exited.

title: Set up worktree on a feature branch
description:
Create an isolated worktree checked out on a new feature branch off
base_branch. The feature branch will be pushed and turned into a PR
in the terminal step.

**Config: base_branch = main**
**Config: setup_command = **

**1. Fetch latest:**
```bash
git fetch --prune origin
```

**2. Resolve worktree path and branch name from metadata (resume case).**

Check if `metadata.work_dir` already records your worktree path, and if
`metadata.branch` records your feature branch name:
```bash
WORKTREE=$(bd show ga-bxq --json | jq -r '.metadata.work_dir // empty')

```

**If worktree path exists in metadata and directory still exists** — reuse it:
```bash
cd "$WORKTREE"
git fetch origin main
# If branch has unpushed local work, rebase it on base_branch; else stay on branch

```
If the directory is missing, fall through to create a new one.

**If no worktree** — create one:
```bash
# Derive a branch name if not already recorded
if [ -z "$BRANCH" ]; then
    SLUG=$(echo "ga-bxq" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | tr -c 'a-z0-9-' '-' | sed 's/-\+/-/g; s/^-//; s/-$//')
    BRANCH="users/${GC_AGENT:-polecat}/${SLUG}"
fi

rmdir "$WORKTREE_PATH"
# Create feature branch off latest base_branch, check out in the new worktree
git worktree add -b "$BRANCH" "$WORKTREE_PATH" "origin/main"
cd "$WORKTREE_PATH"
```

Record immediately so restarts and witness recovery can find both:
```bash
bd update ga-bxq --set-metadata work_dir="$WORKTREE_PATH"
bd update ga-bxq --set-metadata branch="$BRANCH"
```

```bash
git status                  # Should be clean
```

title: mol-polecat-pr
description:
Polecat PR-review variant — commits to a feature branch and opens a pull
request against base_branch.

Extends mol-polecat-base with a feature-branch workspace setup (isolated
/tmp worktree + branch off base_branch) and a commit-push-PR terminal
step instead of direct-to-base commit. Designed for cities that use
human or automated PR review (e.g., MCDClient) before merge.

## Polecat Contract (PR-Review Model)

1. Receive work (molecule poured with this formula, assigned to you)
2. Follow steps in order (read descriptions, execute, move to next)
3. Commit to feature branch, push, open PR, close bead, exit
4. You are GONE — review + merge happens downstream

**Feature branch + PR.** Work lands on `users/<agent>/<issue-slug>`
branch in a /tmp worktree that is isolated from the shared rig tree.
The PR is opened against base_branch via `gh pr create`.

## Why /tmp worktrees

Pool agents sharing a single rig working tree stomp each other: one
agent's `git checkout <branch>` wipes another agent's untracked files
or changes HEAD out from under them. An isolated `/tmp/polecat-*`
worktree per issue removes the stomping entirely.

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Tests fail | Fix them. Do not proceed with failures. |
| Push fails | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| `gh pr create` fails | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| Blocked on external | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| Context filling | `gc runtime request-restart` (blocks until controller kills you) |
| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |

4. doSlingBatch routing+display+dry-run mixed (cmd_sling.go ~616-674)
5. newConvergeCreateCmd ad-hoc var parsing (cmd_converge.go ~54-105)

**Working principles:**
- Follow existing codebase conventions
- Make atomic, focused commits
- Keep changes scoped to the assigned issue
- Don't gold-plate or scope-creep

**If resuming a rejected branch:** Read `metadata.rejection_reason`
from load-context. Focus on fixing the specific issue that caused
rejection — don't redo everything.

**Commit frequently:**
```bash
git add <files>
git commit -m "<type>: <description> (ga-mat)"
```

Commit types: feat, fix, refactor, test, docs, chore

**Discovered work (outside scope):**
```bash
bd create --title "Found: <description>" --type bug --priority 2
```
Do NOT fix unrelated issues in this branch.

**If stuck (>15 minutes):**
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Stuck on implementation" -m "Issue: ga-mat
Problem: <what's blocking you>
Tried: <what you've attempted>"
```

**If context filling up:**
```bash
gc runtime request-restart
```
This blocks until the controller kills your session. The next session
resumes from context (re-reads formula steps, checks git/bead state).

**Exit criteria:** Implementation complete, all changes committed.

title: Verify pre-flights pass on base branch
description:
Check if the codebase is healthy BEFORE starting your work.

**Config: typecheck_command = **
**Config: lint_command = **
**Config: test_command = **

**Skip this step if resuming a rejected branch** — pre-flights were
already verified on the prior attempt. Close this step and proceed.

**1. Run pre-flights (skip empty commands silently):**
```bash



```

**2. If pre-flights pass:** proceed.

**3. If pre-flights fail on main:**

File a bead and proceed. Do NOT fix pre-existing failures — that's
not your assignment.

FORBIDDEN: Pushing to main. FORBIDDEN: Fixing pre-existing failures.

```bash
bd create --title "Pre-existing failure: <description>" --type bug --priority 1
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "NOTICE: main has failing pre-flights" -m "Filed: <bead-id>. Proceeding with ga-mat."
```

**Exit criteria:** Pre-flights pass (or pre-existing bug filed), ready to implement.

title: Set up worktree on a feature branch
description:
Create an isolated worktree checked out on a new feature branch off
base_branch. The feature branch will be pushed and turned into a PR
in the terminal step.

**Config: base_branch = main**
**Config: setup_command = **

**1. Fetch latest:**
```bash
git fetch --prune origin
```

**2. Resolve worktree path and branch name from metadata (resume case).**

Check if `metadata.work_dir` already records your worktree path, and if
`metadata.branch` records your feature branch name:
```bash
WORKTREE=$(bd show ga-mat --json | jq -r '.metadata.work_dir // empty')
BRANCH=$(bd show ga-mat --json | jq -r '.metadata.branch // empty')
```

**If worktree path exists in metadata and directory still exists** — reuse it:
```bash
cd "$WORKTREE"
git fetch origin main
# If branch has unpushed local work, rebase it on base_branch; else stay on branch
git rebase origin/main
```
If the directory is missing, fall through to create a new one.

**If no worktree** — create one:
```bash
# Derive a branch name if not already recorded
if [ -z "$BRANCH" ]; then
    SLUG=$(echo "ga-mat" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | tr -c 'a-z0-9-' '-' | sed 's/-\+/-/g; s/^-//; s/-$//')
    BRANCH="users/${GC_AGENT:-polecat}/${SLUG}"
fi

WORKTREE_PATH=$(mktemp -d /tmp/polecat-ga-mat-XXXXXX)
rmdir "$WORKTREE_PATH"
# Create feature branch off latest base_branch, check out in the new worktree
git worktree add -b "$BRANCH" "$WORKTREE_PATH" "origin/main"
cd "$WORKTREE_PATH"
```

Record immediately so restarts and witness recovery can find both:
```bash
bd update ga-mat --set-metadata work_dir="$WORKTREE_PATH"
bd update ga-mat --set-metadata branch="$BRANCH"
```

**3. Ensure clean working state:**
```bash
git status                  # Should be clean
git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD   # Should show your feature branch
```

**4. Run project setup (if configured):**
```bash

```
Empty setup_command → skip.

**Exit criteria:** In your `/tmp/polecat-*` worktree, on your feature
branch, deps installed, work_dir + branch recorded on the bead.

title: Load context and verify assignment
description:
Initialize your session and understand your assignment.

**1. Prime your environment:**
```bash
gc prime                    # Load role context
bd prime                    # Load beads context
```

**2. Check your hook:**
```bash
bd list --assignee=$GC_AGENT --status=in_progress
```

The hook_bead is your assigned issue. Read it carefully:
```bash
bd show ga-mat           # Full issue details
bd show ga-mat --json | jq '.metadata'  # Check for existing metadata
```

**3. Check for rejection (IMPORTANT):**

If `metadata.rejection_reason` exists, this bead was previously attempted
and rejected by the refinery. Read the reason carefully:
- Rebase conflict → you'll resume the existing branch and rebase
- Test failure → you'll resume the branch and fix the issue

If `metadata.branch` exists, a branch already exists from the prior attempt.
You will use it in workspace-setup instead of creating a new one.

**4. Check inbox for additional context:**
```bash
gc mail inbox
# Read any HANDOFF or assignment messages, then archive after absorbing context
# gc mail read <id> → process → gc mail archive <id>
```

**5. Understand the requirements:**
- What exactly needs to be done?
- What files are likely involved?
- Are there dependencies or blockers?
- What does "done" look like?
- If rejected: what specifically needs fixing?

If blocked or unclear, mail Witness:
```bash
gc mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Unclear requirements" -m "Issue: ga-mat
Question: <what you need clarified>"
```

**Exit criteria:** You understand the work and can begin.

title: mol-polecat-pr
description:
Polecat PR-review variant — commits to a feature branch and opens a pull
request against base_branch.

Extends mol-polecat-base with a feature-branch workspace setup (isolated
/tmp worktree + branch off base_branch) and a commit-push-PR terminal
step instead of direct-to-base commit. Designed for cities that use
human or automated PR review (e.g., MCDClient) before merge.

## Polecat Contract (PR-Review Model)

1. Receive work (molecule poured with this formula, assigned to you)
2. Follow steps in order (read descriptions, execute, move to next)
3. Commit to feature branch, push, open PR, close bead, exit
4. You are GONE — review + merge happens downstream

**Feature branch + PR.** Work lands on `users/<agent>/<issue-slug>`
branch in a /tmp worktree that is isolated from the shared rig tree.
The PR is opened against base_branch via `gh pr create`.

## Why /tmp worktrees

Pool agents sharing a single rig working tree stomp each other: one
agent's `git checkout <branch>` wipes another agent's untracked files
or changes HEAD out from under them. An isolated `/tmp/polecat-*`
worktree per issue removes the stomping entirely.

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Tests fail | Fix them. Do not proceed with failures. |
| Push fails | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| `gh pr create` fails | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| Blocked on external | Mail Witness, mark yourself stuck |
| Context filling | `gc runtime request-restart` (blocks until controller kills you) |
| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |

title: gc BEADS_ACTOR inconsistent across session spawn paths
description:
**Symptom**: `BEADS_ACTOR` (what `bd update --claim` uses for the assignee) has different formats depending on how the session was spawned:

| Session origin | GC_SESSION_NAME / BEADS_ACTOR | Example |
|----------------|--------------------------------|---------|
| ephemeral (pool) | `<template-basename>-<session-id>` | `designer-lgf-b807` |
| manual (user-spawned named) | `s-<session-id>` | `s-lgf-jtvr` |
| named always-on (singleton) | `<rig>/<template>` sometimes | `claude-multiplex/witness` |

**Why it matters**:

1. Cross-session recovery breaks — an agent's tier-1 query `bd list --assignee=$GC_TEMPLATE` won't find beads the session previously claimed if `--claim` stored the assignee as `s-lgf-jtvr`.
2. Bead history becomes cryptic — `s-lgf-jtvr` tells a future reader nothing about the agent's role.
3. Pack authors have to guess which identity to use in prompts ($GC_TEMPLATE vs $BEADS_ACTOR vs $GC_AGENT). Today the prompt templates assume $GC_TEMPLATE is consistent — it is — but claims go via $BEADS_ACTOR which ISN'T.

**Desired outcome**: single convention for BEADS_ACTOR across all spawn paths. For any scope=rig singleton agent (max_active_sessions=1), ideal is BEADS_ACTOR == $GC_TEMPLATE (e.g. `claude-multiplex/builder`). For pool agents, could be `<template>-<N>` or `<template>-<session-id>` — but predictable and documented.

**Repro**:

    # Pool spawn path (ephemeral)
    pgrep -af '\[l1-gc-factory\] claude-multiplex/designer' | head -1 \
        | xargs -I{} cat /proc/{}/environ | tr '\0' '\n' | grep BEADS_ACTOR
    # Manual spawn path
    pgrep -af '\[l1-gc-factory\] claude-multiplex/builder' | head -1 \
        | xargs -I{} cat /proc/{}/environ | tr '\0' '\n' | grep BEADS_ACTOR

Both are reading from a running session, both are scope=rig agents in the same rig, but the BEADS_ACTOR format differs.

**Related fix already landed**: pack prompts were updated to `bd update <id> --claim --assignee="$GC_TEMPLATE"` so claims pin assignee to the role. That assumes `--claim` respects the `--assignee` override — if it DOESN'T, that fix is a no-op and this bug is the only path forward.

**Low priority** — deferred until network/env is less noisy. Not blocking anything urgent.

**Note**: also worth flagging (but don't investigate now per human's call) — a sling to `l1-gc-factory/claude-multiplex/builder` didn't auto-spawn the session; user had to wake it manually. That may be related (pool budget, route caching) or unrelated. Record it here, investigate later.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Single BEADS_ACTOR convention documented across spawn paths. 2) For singleton named sessions (max_active=1), BEADS_ACTOR == GC_TEMPLATE. 3) --claim --assignee behavior documented. 4) Root cause located in gc code (spawn path that sets SESSION_NAME / BEADS_ACTOR).
notes: Investigation complete. Root cause: cmd/gc/template_resolve.go:223 sets BEADS_ACTOR=sessName once. The named-session overrides in build_desired_state.go:386-389 and session_reconciler.go:1095-1098 rewrite GC_AGENT/GC_ALIAS/GC_TEMPLATE to the raw identity but leave BEADS_ACTOR pointing at the sanitized sessName, so in a named session BEADS_ACTOR != GC_AGENT. Verified empirically in this investigator session (BEADS_ACTOR=gascity--investigator, GC_AGENT=gascity/investigator). Also confirmed empirically that 'bd update --claim --assignee=X' honors X (overrides BEADS_ACTOR default). Fix handed to builder via ga-6fo.

title: Replace per-tick fingerprint recomputation with config-reload diff
description:
## Context

The reconciler's config-drift detection compares a stored fingerprint
(computed at session start) against a per-tick recomputation via
`runtime.CoreFingerprint(templateParamsToConfig(tp))`. The recomputation
depends on transient I/O — specifically `LoadCityCatalog` reading the
filesystem every tick in `newAgentBuildParams`. This produces
non-deterministic fingerprints when:

- `LoadCityCatalog` returns an error (filesystem race, permissions flap).
- `LoadCityCatalog` returns an empty catalog despite the cfg referencing
  skills (observed in gc-management — cfg state briefly flaps during a
  config-reload window).

PR #910 patches this with a process-level last-good cache: a load failure
or empty result reuses the prior cached catalog so the fingerprint stays
stable. This is load-bearing for session stability (without it, every
live session drains in a config-drift storm every few ticks) but it's a
workaround for a deeper design flaw.

## Proposal

Fingerprints should be deterministic functions of *declared* config,
never of transient I/O. The `CityRuntime.reloadConfig` path already
tracks a `configRev` revision that's stable within a reload cycle; drift
detection should hang off that instead of per-tick hash recomputation.

Sketch:

1. On session start, record the full `TemplateParams` (or a hash of just
   the identity-relevant fields) in session metadata — same as today.
2. On config reload success, diff old vs new `config.City` for
   identity-relevant fields (Command, included Env keys, WakeMode, pool
   bounds, DependsOn, skill entries, CopyFiles ContentHash). Mark
   affected session templates as "needs restart".
3. Reconciler reads the "needs restart" flag, drains affected sessions,
   clears the flag.
4. No per-tick hash recomputation. No filesystem I/O in the drift path.

## Benefits

- Fingerprinting is no longer I/O-dependent → no more "transient failure
  drains everything" storms.
- One diff computation per reload instead of one fingerprint per session
  per tick (performance).
- Clearer semantics: drift fires when config *actually changed*, not when
  the filesystem briefly hiccupped.
- Eliminates the whole skill-catalog-cache workaround.

## Related

- PR #910 — cache workaround that ships for 1.0
- PR #833 — dropped pool.check from FingerprintExtra (same class of bug,
  narrower fix)
- PR #869 — ignored GC_ALIAS (same class, narrower)
- Open issue #460 — "Pool scale-up from min=0 never spawns polecats for
  labeled work + config-drift drain storm (ga-7xq)"

## Scope / timing

Target post-1.0 — this is a structural cleanup, not a stability fix.
#910 already resolves the user-visible breakage.

value: bd create --deps 'blocked-by:X' stores a dep of TYPE blocked-by which bd ready IGNORES as a blocker. Only deps of TYPE blocks (from 'bd dep add CHILD PARENT' or --deps 'blocks:X' where the new bead is the blocker) are respected by bd ready. When child-blocked-by-parent relationships are needed for readiness gating, always use 'bd dep add <dependent> <prerequisite>' after create, or use --deps 'blocks:<new-dependent-id>' at create time on the prerequisite.
